title: Hard Fork to Protocol Version 11 ('van Rossem' Hard Fork)

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this HardForkInitiation (enacted), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The action upgrades Cardano Mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to protocol version 11.0 without an era transition." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide independent evidence for: UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic; Provide independent evidence for: Security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives. Residual uncertainty: Protocol action lacks a complete independently pinned readiness packet: security_review_pass Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 50.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is above the hard-fork threshold; treated as a positive context signal.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic.
Provide independent evidence for: Security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Initiate an intra-era hard fork of Cardano Mainnet to Protocol Version 11.0, called the van Rossem hard fork.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Upgrade Cardano mainnet protocol to major version 11 and minor version 0 while remaining in the Conway era., Make new Plutus primitives from CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153 available., Make all Plutus built-in functions consistently available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3., Add UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data., Promote or tighten ledger and node validation, diagnostics, and security rules including VRF key hash uniqueness and PPView mismatch reporting.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The action upgrades Cardano Mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to protocol version 11.0 without an era transition.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The upgrade introduces new Plutus primitives defined by CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): All Plutus built-in functions, including newly introduced ones, will become available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The hard fork promotes VRF key hash uniqueness to a ledger-level rule so no two stake pools can register or operate with the same VRF key.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Testing reports demonstrate no behavioral regressions, complete conformance between specification and implementation for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
missing: Independent evidence for: UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic.

Independent evidence for: Testing reports demonstrate no behavioral regressions, complete conformance between specification and implementation for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
Independent evidence for: Security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The action upgrades Cardano Mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to protocol version 11.0 without an era transition." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will provide significant performance improvements and cleaner script logic." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.
